description_long: This object is a stylized flower with a tall, slender green stem supporting a single bloom. The bloom consists of a central, purple, bud-like structure surrounded by two distinct whorls of pointed green sepals or bracts, giving it a layered, spiky appearance. The petals are not individually distinct but appear as a compact, geometric cluster at the center. The green foliage radiates symmetrically around the central bud, creating a star-like effect. The stem is straight and cylindrical, with no visible branching or additional leaves. The overall form is simple and idealized, resembling a thistle or lotus in its upright, solitary presentation.
description: A stylized flower with a tall green stem and a central purple bud surrounded by pointed green foliage. The bloom is compact and symmetrical, with a spiky, layered appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central purple bud and symmetrical green foliage radiating outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered, spiky arrangement of the green foliage and the upright stem.
description_view_2: Rear view with the green foliage appearing more prominent and the purple bud partially obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with the purple bud visible and the green foliage forming a star-like pattern.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration, with two distinct layers of pointed green sepals or bracts radiating symmetrically around a central purple bud. The arrangement is highly regular and star-like, with overlapping and layering present.
